top_stocks.description:
• Alamos Gold Inc. is a precious-metals mining company focused primarily on producing gold, with operations centered in Canada and Mexico (and related development projects/expansions). It is in the gold mining industry.
• Market cap and size classification: Use web sources to determine the current market-cap tier (large/mid/small). If you want, I can classify by (a) current market cap and (b) typical index membership (S&P/TSX) once you confirm the exact universe you want (NYSE vs TSX listing).
• Key competitive advantage/position: Scale and operational track record across multiple districts/mines, plus near-/medium-term production and cost outlooks tied to specific expansion projects (e.g., growth initiatives and mine-life support).
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-04 (reported by the company as part of its year-end reporting cycle): Alamos Gold reported fourth-quarter and year-end 2025 results, including commentary around production outlook and growth drivers, and announced a 60% increase in its dividend.
• 2026-04 (same reporting release): The company discussed outlook tied to first full year of production from the Puerto de la Cruz / PDA-related pipeline and continued low-cost production improvements in the Island Gold District with expansion completion.
• 2026-02 (earlier in the same 2025 reporting arc, with long-horizon follow-through): Alamos published three-year operating guidance targeting production growth by 2028 at significantly lower costs, outlining project milestones and financing/self-funding expectations for growth.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=22.81 and PEG=0.32 suggest a relatively reasonable price relative to expected growth for a gold miner; however, gold stocks are highly sensitive to realized gold prices, margins, and capital intensity.
• Profitability: ROE=0.2206% is modest, but ROIC=19.02% is meaningfully stronger; profit margin is ~0.4897%. For miners, reported margins can look compressed versus software/semis, so ROIC tends to be a more informative quality check.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4128% is comparatively low/steady rather than high-growth, so the main “growth” case relies more on operating execution, sustaining capital efficiency, and production/cost trajectory rather than rapid EPS compounding.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 implies strong composite quality in your screener; with these metrics, the key question is whether management can sustain the cost/production program without commodity-driven drawdowns.

top_stocks.description:
• Broadcom Inc. is a diversified semiconductor and infrastructure software company. It competes in custom silicon/accelerators, networking/AI infrastructure components, and enterprise infrastructure software (including virtualization and operations tooling via VMware).
• Market cap and size classification: Broadcom is typically categorized as a mega-cap; confirm current market cap tier via your preferred reference (NYSE: AVGO).
• Key competitive advantage/position: Scale and integration advantages from its semiconductor + infrastructure software platform; strong AI infrastructure relevance (custom silicon and networking) alongside enterprise software recurring revenue potential.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-04 (approx.; reported as fiscal Q1 2026 results coverage): Broadcom reported a record fiscal Q1 revenue figure (often cited around $19.3B in media recaps) with AI-related revenue momentum and an outlook for subsequent-quarter consolidated revenue.
• 2026-04-07 to 2026-04-08 (reported by markets coverage): Follow-on strategic/AI partnership deal headlines contributed to upward stock moves in mid-April 2026 coverage.
• 2026-04 (recent coverage): Ongoing analyst commentary and price-target changes highlighted expectations for AI-driven demand and infrastructure software momentum.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=77.37 and PEG=0.45 indicate a premium multiple. PEG below 1 suggests that, relative to growth assumptions in your model, valuation may be more defensible than the raw P/E implies—though investors should still watch for execution risk and AI-cycle normalization.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3337% is low numerically while ROIC=17.38% is solid; profit margin ~0.3657% is consistent with how large-cap semis often show diluted margins at scale. The key is whether ROIC remains resilient.
• Growth: EPS this Y=0.6589% and next Y=0.5868% are relatively modest; next 5Y=0.4864% also points to steady (not explosive) compounding. The “why own” case is typically durability of cash flows and AI-infrastructure relevance rather than rapid EPS acceleration.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 supports a strong overall profile in your screener; with a high P/E, quality must translate into durable earnings power and continued buybacks/capital returns.

top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology, Inc. is a memory and storage semiconductor manufacturer (DRAM, NAND flash, and related memory technologies) serving data centers, PCs, and mobile devices.
• Market cap and size classification: Micron is generally a large-cap semiconductor stock; confirm the current market-cap bucket using your preferred cutoff rules.
• Key competitive advantage/position: Cost-curve and technology execution in memory (including high-bandwidth memory adoption trends), plus supply discipline that can tighten industry fundamentals when demand accelerates.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-18: Micron reported results for its second quarter of fiscal 2026 (quarter ended 2026-02-26). Public company materials and coverage emphasized record-setting metrics and strong demand conditions.
• 2026-03-18: In that same quarter results release, Micron declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share, payable on 2026-04-15 to shareholders of record as of 2026-03-30.
• 2026-03-18 (guidance/forward look): Management provided revenue and margin/EPS guidance for the following fiscal quarter, reflecting expectations for continued strength and significant year-over-year improvement.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=21.54 with PEG=0.04 is extremely attractive in your model. This usually implies that your EPS growth expectations are high relative to price, but it also warrants caution: memory companies can be cyclical, and peak earnings don’t always persist.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3982% is low-to-moderate numerically, while ROIC=29.16% is strong; profit margin ~0.4149% is plausible for semis where margins fluctuate with pricing and utilization.
• Growth: EPS this Y=5.8604% is strong; next Y=0.6565% and next 5Y=1.1387% look more normalized, which fits memory cycles. The key is whether your “cycle peak” assumption is driving most of the forward valuation logic.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 suggests excellent overall composite quality in your screener; with MU, confirm that the recent cycle tailwind (pricing/mix) isn’t being over-weighted.

top_stocks.description:
• NVIDIA Corporation designs GPUs and accelerated computing platforms for AI, data centers, and high-performance computing. It is a leading provider of AI training/inference infrastructure.
• Market cap and size classification: NVIDIA is a mega-cap.
• Key competitive advantage/position: CUDA/software ecosystem and end-to-end platform approach (hardware + networking + systems), plus strong product leadership across major AI generations (e.g., Blackwell-era ramp themes).
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-17 (GTC coverage): NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang stated the company has received export licenses for multiple Chinese customers for H200 GPUs and is in the process of restarting H200 manufacturing for shipments to China.
• 2026-03 (recent financial reporting cycle coverage): NVIDIA’s latest quarterly results materials emphasized strong revenue growth and very large data-center revenue contribution, with detailed GAAP and non-GAAP figures published by NVIDIA.
• 2026-03 (continued strategic commentary): Media and market intelligence coverage tied the outlook to ongoing demand for AI infrastructure and commentary around Blackwell and related supply-chain readiness.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=40.57 and PEG=0.45 show a premium valuation, but the PEG suggests growth is expected to support part of that premium. For NVDA, valuation sensitivity to AI demand and supply-chain execution is high.
• Profitability: ROE=1.0149% is strong, ROIC=71.75% is exceptionally high, and profit margin=0.556% (as provided by your screener) supports the view of high profitability—though ensure the margin input is correctly defined (GAAP vs non-GAAP and how your screener scales it).
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.3956% is modest relative to NVDA’s historical volatility; with NVDA, investors often underwrite growth via platform adoption and long-duration AI spend rather than steady low single-digit EPS compounding.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 aligns with a “quality compounder” profile (strong ROIC, ecosystem strength). The main diligence point is whether forward growth assumptions in your model match the AI cycle regime.

top_stocks.description:
• Centerra Gold Inc. is a gold mining company with operations and development projects, typically focused on producing gold and also managing portfolio-level optionality across multiple mineral assets.
• Market cap and size classification: CGAU is typically a mid-cap gold producer in market classifications; confirm current tier using market cap on 2026-04-15.
• Key competitive advantage/position: Portfolio diversification across producing mines plus a self-funded growth strategy approach in recent guidance cycles; the key is execution of expansion/development while managing costs and balance-sheet risk.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-19: Centerra Gold reported fourth quarter and full year 2025 results and issued 2026 guidance, describing robust annual production, cost guidance, and an outlook that remains strong.
• 2026-02-19 (strategy/growth): The company emphasized a self-funded growth strategy tied to executing its growth pipeline and maintaining production/cost discipline.
• 2026-02 (cost/production execution commentary): Coverage and company reporting highlighted cost guidance/beat context and ongoing operational performance drivers that support 2026 outlook.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=6.81 and PEG=0.43 imply a low-cost-of-earnings relative to expected growth. For gold producers, low P/E can be rational if the market expects commodity risk, dilution, or weaker forward earnings—so validate whether guidance supports earnings durability.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3143% is modest while ROIC=28.20% is strong; profit margin ~0.4469% is supportive for a miner, though miners’ margins vary significantly with realized prices and sustaining capital.
• Growth: EPS this Y=0.8204% and next Y=0.0467% are relatively low, while next 5Y=0.2129% is also modest—suggesting a more value/turnaround/quality-with-leverage profile rather than a high-growth compounder.
• Quality: Investor Score=95/100 indicates high overall quality in your screener. With CGAU specifically, diligence should focus on execution risk of the growth plan and sensitivity to gold price realizations and cost inflation.
